@@ -1,48 +1,50 @@
 # Phase 01: Running Agents Orchestration Prototype
 
 This phase delivers the first end-to-end orchestration prototype for GatomIA by turning the existing running-agent, agent-chat, and cloud-agent surfaces into a single coherent experience that visibly tracks active sessions. It matters because it gives Product Engineers and Software Engineers a working orchestration dashboard immediately, proves the new direction with minimal refactoring risk, and creates the foundation that later phases will extend with hooks, visual flows, and autonomous task execution.
 
 - NEVER push de code
 
 ## Tasks
 
 - [x] Establish the orchestration prototype baseline by reusing existing extension and webview surfaces before creating new ones:
   - Inspect and map the current behavior in `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts`, `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts`, `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts`, `src/panels/agent-chat-panel.ts`, `src/panels/cloud-agent-progress-panel.ts`, and `ui/src/components/cloud-agents/cloud-agent-progress-view.tsx`
   - Identify the smallest viable prototype that can be built by composing existing session state, registry events, and view registration instead of introducing a second orchestration stack
   - Capture the target slice in `docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md` using Obsidian-style markdown with YAML front matter, links to `[[Running-Agents-Model]]` and `[[Orchestration-UI-Contract]]`, and explicit notes about reused modules versus new modules
   - Note: baseline mapping and reuse-first target slice captured on 2026-04-30 in `docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md`
 
 - [x] Implement a unified orchestration read model for active and recent agents:
   - Reuse existing session and provider contracts before creating new types; search for agent session models, lifecycle state helpers, and existing telemetry patterns first
   - Create or refactor a narrow service that merges local running-agent sessions and cloud-agent session progress into one normalized projection for the UI
   - Preserve current tree-view behavior while exposing the same normalized data to a webview-friendly bridge so the prototype has one source of truth
   - Include lifecycle metadata that is already available or derivable now: session id, source, title, agent name, state, timestamps, worktree status, last visible activity, and blocking status
   - Note: completed on 2026-04-30 by extending `src/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.ts` to merge persisted and live agent-chat sessions, derive transcript-backed titles/activity, and normalize provider display names for cloud sessions without changing existing tree providers.
   - Validation: re-verified on 2026-04-30 with `tests/unit/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.test.ts` and `npm run check`; the check required clearing a disposable oversized `.entire/tmp` session artifact before passing.
 
 - [x] Build the first orchestration prototype view that visibly works inside the extension:
   - Reuse the existing page registry, panel, and bridge patterns before introducing new routing or state libraries
   - Add a dedicated orchestration view or panel that renders the normalized session projection with clear columns or cards for Active, Waiting, Completed, and Failed work
   - Ensure the prototype allows at minimum: open session, refresh session state, inspect current status, and navigate back to the existing agent-chat or cloud-agent experience
   - Keep the UI intentionally simple but visually compelling so the user can see live orchestration progress as soon as the phase is complete
   - Note: completed on 2026-04-30 by wiring the existing orchestration webview provider into the extension bootstrap, adding a simple four-lane card UI in `ui/src/features/orchestration/index.tsx`, and fixing cloud-session navigation so the prototype can jump back to the Cloud Agents surface instead of only refreshing state.
   - Validation: re-verified on 2026-04-30 with `tests/unit/webview/cloud-agent-progress-view.test.tsx` and `npm run check`.
 
 - [x] Connect orchestration events, telemetry, and empty states so the prototype behaves like a real product surface:
   - Reuse existing event emitters, output channels, and telemetry helpers before adding new instrumentation paths
   - Make tree updates and orchestration view updates respond to session-store, registry, and cloud-provider changes without manual reloads where possible
   - Add actionable empty, loading, and degraded states that explain whether there are no sessions, missing providers, or failed status reads
   - Write implementation notes in `docs/architecture/orchestration/orchestration-ui-contract.md` with YAML front matter and wiki-links to `[[Running-Agents-Prototype]]`
   - Note: completed on 2026-04-30 by wiring orchestration refreshes to existing `AgentSessionStorage` and `ProviderRegistry` change events, replacing the prototype's timer-based provider refresh path, and upgrading the orchestration webview with actionable no-session, missing-provider, and degraded-state messaging.
   - Validation: verified on 2026-04-30 with `tests/unit/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.test.ts`, `tests/unit/webview/cloud-agent-progress-view.test.tsx`, and `npm run check`.
 
-- [ ] Write test coverage for the orchestration prototype behavior:
+- [x] Write test coverage for the orchestration prototype behavior:
   - Add or update unit tests for the normalized orchestration read model, lifecycle grouping, and any new state selectors or adapters
   - Add webview or provider-focused tests that verify the prototype renders meaningful empty, active, and failed states from representative session data
   - Prefer extending existing test suites around providers and stores before creating entirely new harnesses
+  - Note: completed on 2026-04-30 by adding `tests/unit/providers/orchestration-view-provider.test.ts` to exercise snapshot delivery, bridge actions, and degraded fallback behavior without creating a separate orchestration harness, then re-verifying the existing orchestration read-model and webview suites.
+  - Validation: verified on 2026-04-30 with `npm test -- tests/unit/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.test.ts`, `npm test -- tests/unit/webview/cloud-agent-progress-view.test.tsx`, `npm test -- tests/unit/providers/orchestration-view-provider.test.ts`, and `npm run check`.
 
 - [ ] Run the orchestration quality gates and fix failures until the prototype is working end-to-end:
   - Run the relevant targeted tests for orchestration changes first
   - Run `npm run check`
   - Run the full affected test command if targeted tests expose regressions that require broader validation
   - Verify the extension can surface the new orchestration prototype without breaking the existing Running Agents and Cloud Agents views

"# Context

- **Agent Path:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode
- **Git Branch:** 018-agent-chat-panel
- **Auto Run Folder:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs
- **Loop Iteration:** 00001
- **Working Folder for Temporary Files:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs/Working

If you need to create the working folder, do so.

---

## CRITICAL: Response Format Requirement

**Your response MUST begin with a specific, actionable synopsis of what you accomplished.**

- GOOD examples: \"Added pagination to the user list component\", \"Fixed authentication timeout bug in login.ts\", \"Refactored database queries to use prepared statements\"
- BAD examples: \"The task is complete\", \"Task completed successfully\", \"Done\", \"Finished the task\"

The synopsis is displayed in the History panel and must describe the actual work done, not just that work was done.

---

## Structured Output Artifacts

When creating documentation, research notes, reports, or any knowledge artifacts (not source code), use **structured Markdown** by default:

### YAML Front Matter

```yaml
---
type: research | note | report | analysis | reference
title: Descriptive Title
created: YYYY-MM-DD
tags:
  - relevant-tag
related:
  - '[[Other-Document]]'
---
```

### Wiki-Link Cross-References

Use `[[Document-Name]]` syntax to connect related documents. This enables graph exploration in Maestro's DocGraph viewer and tools like Obsidian.

### Folder Organization

Organize artifacts in logical folders by entity type or domain:

```
docs/
├── research/
│   ├── topic-a.md
│   └── topic-b.md
├── architecture/
│   └── system-design.md
└── decisions/
    └── adr-001-choice.md
```

**When to apply:** Research findings, competitive analysis, architecture decisions, technical specs, meeting notes, reference docs, glossaries.

**When NOT to apply:** Source code files, config files (JSON/YAML), generated assets, temporary files.

## Instructions

1. Project Orientation
   Begin by reviewing CLAUDE.md (when available) in this folder to understand the project's structure, conventions, and workflow expectations.

2. Task Selection
   Process the FIRST unchecked task (- [ ]) from top to bottom. Note that there may be relevant images associated with the task. If there are, analyze them, and include in your final synopsis back how many images you analyzed in preparation for solving the task.

   IMPORTANT: You will only work on this single task. If the task description contains multiple steps or subtasks (e.g., bullet points, numbered lists, or semicolon-separated items), treat them as one cohesive unit—they were intentionally grouped by the document author to share context. Complete all parts before marking done. Do not move on to the next major checkbox item.

3. Task Evaluation
   - Fully understand the task and inspect the relevant code.
   - Identify all subtasks within the current checkbox item.
   - There will be future runs to take care of other checkbox items.

4. Task Implementation
   - **Before creating new code**, search for existing implementations, utilities, helpers, or patterns in the codebase that can be reused or extended. Avoid duplicating functionality that already exists.
   - Implement the task according to the project's established style, architecture, and coding norms.
   - Ensure that test cases are created, and that they pass.
   - Ensure you haven't broken any existing test cases.

5. Completion + Reporting
   - Mark the task as completed by changing \"- [ ]\" to \"- [x]\".
   - Begin your response with the specific synopsis (see \"Response Format Requirement\" above).
   - Follow with any relevant details about:
     - Implementation approach or key decisions made
     - Why the task was intentionally skipped (if applicable)
     - If implementation failed, explain the failure and do NOT check off the item.

6. Version Control
   For any code or documentation changes, if we're in a Github repo:
   - Commit using a descriptive message prefixed with \"MAESTRO: \".
   - Push to GitHub.
   - Update CLAUDE.md, README.md, or any other top-level documentation if appropriate.

7. Exit Immediately
   After completing (or skipping) your task, EXIT. Do not proceed to additional tasks—another agent instance will handle them. If there are no remaining open tasks, exit immediately and state that there is nothing left to do.

---

## Tasks

Process tasks from this document:

/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs/Initiation/2026-04-30-gatomia-orchestration-playbook/GATOMIA-ORCHESTRATION-01.md

Check off tasks and add any relevant notes around the completion directly within that document.
"
Reviewing project guidance and the playbook task first, then I’ll implement only the first open checkbox item and verify it end to end.

1: # Repository Guidelines
2: 
3: This file provides guidance to AI agents when working with code in this repository.
4: 
5: ## MANDATORY ACTIONS
6: 
7: - Follow Visual Studio Code Extension Guidelines provided by Microsoft present at [https://code.visualstudio.com/api/extension-guides/overview](https://code.visualstudio.com/api/extension-guides/overview)
8: - Follow Visual Studio Code UX guidelines provided by Microsoft present at [https://code.visualstudio.com/api/ux-guidelines/overview](https://code.visualstudio.com/api/ux-guidelines/overview)
9: - Follow GitHub Copilot SDK guidelines provided by GitHub present at [https://github.com/github/copilot-sdk](https://github.com/github/copilot-sdk)
10: - Always run `npm run check` before marking any task as complete. This ensures code quality and formatting standards are met.
11: - Never use emoji in source code files (from global user instructions).
12: 
13: ## Project Constitution (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
14: 
15: This project follows strict principles defined in [.specify/memory/constitution.md](.specify/memory/constitution.md):
16: 
17: ### I. Kebab-Case File Naming (MANDATORY)
18: 
19: - **All source files MUST use kebab-case** (e.g., `preview-store.ts`, `document-preview-panel.ts`)
20: - ❌ NO camelCase, PascalCase, or snake_case files
21: - Linter will fail on non-compliant filenames
22: - Exceptions: Config files requiring specific naming (`package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `README.md`)
23: 
24: ### II. TypeScript-First Development
25: 
26: - All code MUST be TypeScript with `strict: true`
27: - No `any` types without explicit justification
28: - All public APIs MUST have complete type definitions
29: 
30: ### III. Test-First Development (TDD)
31: 
32: - Tests MUST be written BEFORE implementation
33: - Red-Green-Refactor cycle strictly enforced
34: - PRs without tests are rejected
35: - Test coverage MUST NOT decrease
36: - Integration tests required for new features
37: 
38: ### IV. Observability & Instrumentation
39: 
40: - Significant operations MUST include telemetry, logging, and error reporting
41: - Performance-critical paths MUST include instrumentation
42: - Errors MUST be logged with sufficient debug context
43: - No silent failures
44: 
45: ### V. Simplicity & YAGNI
46: 
47: - Implement only what is needed NOW
48: - No features or abstractions for potential future use
49: - Challenge unnecessary abstractions in code reviews
50: - Refactor when patterns emerge (Rule of Three)
51: 
52: ## Quick Start: Common Commands
53: 
54: ```bash
55: # Install all dependencies (root + ui)
56: npm run install:all
57: 
58: # Development workflow
59: npm run build          # Full build (prompts + extension + webview)
60: npm run watch          # TypeScript watch + webview dev server
61: npm run build-prompts  # Compile markdown prompts to TypeScript
62: npm run build:ext      # Bundle extension only (esbuild)
63: npm run build:webview  # Build React webview (Vite)
64: 
65: # Testing and Quality (MUST pass before commits)
66: npm test               # Run all tests (Vitest)
67: npm run test:watch     # Watch mode for tests
68: npm run test:coverage  # Generate coverage report
69: npm run lint           # Lint with Biome
70: npm run format         # Format code with Biome
71: npm run check          # Run all checks (lint + format validation) - MANDATORY
72: 
73: # Running specific tests
74: npm test -- path/to/test-file.test.ts           # Run single test file
75: npm test -- -t "test name pattern"               # Run tests matching pattern
76: 
77: # Packaging and Publishing
78: npm run package        # Create VSIX file for VS Code Marketplace
79: npm run compile        # Build then package (used for CI/CD)
80: 
81: # Webview development
82: npm --prefix ui run dev  # Run webview dev server in isolation
83: 
84: # Launch extension in debug mode
85: Press F5 in VS Code to launch the Extension Development Host
86: ```
87: 
88: ## Code Quality Standards
89: 
90: ### Formatting & Style
91: 
92: - Tab indentation (configured in formatter)
93: - Double quotes for strings
94: - Semicolons required
95: - `npm run format` MUST pass before commit
96: - `npm run lint` MUST pass before merge
97: 
98: ### Documentation
99: 
100: - Public APIs MUST include JSDoc comments
101: - Complex algorithms MUST include explanatory comments
102: - README files MUST be kept up-to-date with feature changes
103: 
104: ### Error Handling
105: 
106: - All async operations MUST handle errors explicitly
107: - User-facing errors MUST be actionable and clear
108: - Internal errors MUST include sufficient debug context
109: 
110: ## Architecture Overview
111: 
112: ### High-Level Structure
113: 
114: This is a VS Code extension that provides Agentic Spec-Driven Development capabilities, integrating with **SpecKit**, **OpenSpec**, and **GitHub Copilot**.
115: 
116: #### Extension (src/)
117: 
118: - **Main Entry**: `src/extension.ts` - Registers all commands, providers, and services
119: - **Features**: Domain-specific modules (specs, hooks, steering, documents)
120:   - `features/spec/` - Spec management and review flow
121:   - `features/hooks/` - MCP hooks and automation
122:   - `features/steering/` - Constitution/agents management
123:   - `features/documents/` - Document preview and refinement
124: - **Providers**: VS Code Tree Views and UI providers
125:   - `providers/spec-explorer-provider.ts` - Specs tree view
126:   - `providers/hooks-explorer-provider.ts` - Hooks tree view
127:   - `providers/prompts-explorer-provider.ts` - Prompts tree view
128: - **Services**: Core business logic
129:   - `services/prompt-loader.ts` - Loads and compiles markdown prompts
130:   - `services/document-preview-service.ts` - Handles document rendering
131: - **Panels**: Webview panels for rich UI
132:   - `panels/document-preview-panel.ts` - Preview/refinement UI
133: 
134: #### Webview (ui/)
135: 
136: - **React 18** SPA with TypeScript
137: - **Components**: Reusable UI components in `ui/src/components/`
138:   - `spec-explorer/` - Spec review flow components
139:   - `hooks-view/` - Hook management components
140:   - `ui/` - Shared UI components (buttons, forms, etc.)
141: - **Bridge**: VS Code extension ↔ webview communication via `ui/src/bridge/`
142: - **Stores**: State management in `ui/src/stores/`
143: - **Build**: Vite for bundling
144: 
145: ### Key Architectural Patterns
146: 
147: 1. **Dual-Build System**:
148:    - Extension: esbuild (Node.js bundle)
149:    - Webview: Vite (browser bundle)
150:    - Dependencies installed in both root and `ui/` directories
151: 
152: 2. **Message Passing**: Extension communicates with webview via VS Code postMessage API
153:    - Commands sent from webview → extension
154:    - State updates sent from extension → webview
155: 
156: 3. **MCP Integration**: Hooks system integrates with GitHub Copilot's Model Context Protocol
157:    - `MCPDiscoveryService` discovers available MCP servers/tools
158:    - `HookExecutor` executes MCP actions based on triggers
159: 
160: 4. **Review Flow**: Spec lifecycle management with status transitions
161:    - States: draft → review → reopened → archived
162:    - Change requests block archival until addressed
163:    - Telemetry tracks all state transitions
164: 
165: 5. **Test Configuration**: Vitest with dual dependency resolution
166:    - UI dependencies in `ui/node_modules/`
167:    - Testing libraries in root `node_modules/`
168:    - Aliases configured in `vitest.config.ts` to resolve both
169: 
170: ## Development Workflow
171: 
172: ### Adding a New Feature
173: 
174: 1. **Write tests first** (TDD is mandatory)
175:    - Unit tests: `tests/unit/`
176:    - Integration tests: `tests/integration/`
177:    - Webview tests: `ui/tests/` or `tests/unit/webview/`
178: 
179: 2. **Implement feature**
180:    - Follow kebab-case naming
181:    - Use strict TypeScript
182:    - Add telemetry/logging for significant operations
183: 
184: 3. **Before committing**
185: 
186:    ```bash

188:    npm run check      # Linting and formatting must pass
189:    ```
190: 
191: 4. **File a PR**
192:    - Tests included and passing
193:    - Constitution compliance verified
194:    - No test coverage decrease
195: 
196: ### Running Tests for Specific Areas
197: 
198: ```bash
199: # Extension tests
200: npm test -- tests/unit/features/spec/
201: npm test -- tests/integration/
202: 
203: # Webview tests
204: npm test -- ui/tests/
205: npm test -- tests/unit/webview/
206: 
207: # Specific feature
208: npm test -- -t "Review Flow"
209: npm test -- -t "MCP"
210: ```
211: 
212: ## Key Technologies
213: 
214: - **VS Code Extension API**: 1.84.0+
215: - **TypeScript**: 5.3+ (target: ES2022, strict mode)
216: - **React**: 18.3+ (webview only)
217: - **Vitest**: 3.2+ (testing framework)
218: - **Biome**: Linting and formatting
219: - **esbuild**: Extension bundling
220: - **Vite**: Webview bundling
221: - **GitHub Copilot Chat**: AI integration
222: - **MCP (Model Context Protocol)**: Automation hooks
223: 
224: ## Integration Points
225: 
226: ### GitHub Copilot Chat
227: 
228: - Extension sends prompts to Copilot Chat via `sendPromptToChat()`
229: - Prompts compiled from markdown files in `src/prompts/`
230: - Custom instructions configurable per operation type
231: 
232: ### SpecKit / OpenSpec
233: 
234: - Extension detects spec system automatically
235: - Adapters in `utils/spec-kit-adapter.ts` provide unified interface
236: - SpecKit: `.specify/` directory structure
237: - OpenSpec: `openspec/` directory structure
238: 
239: ### MCP Servers
240: 
241: - Discovered via GitHub Copilot Chat configuration
242: - Hooks can trigger MCP tools (create issues, send notifications, etc.)
243: - Graceful degradation when MCP unavailable
244: 
245: ## Hooks System (011-custom-agent-hooks)
246: 
247: ### Overview
248: 
249: The hooks system enables automation by triggering actions when agent operations complete. Hooks can execute before or after agent operations and access captured output data.
250: 
251: ### Key Features
252: 
253: #### Timing Control
254: 
255: - **Before Execution**: Hooks run before agent starts
256:   - Optional blocking: Wait for hook completion before agent proceeds
257:   - Use case: Pre-flight validation, environment setup
258: - **After Execution**: Hooks run after agent completes (default)
259:   - Use case: Notifications, artifact processing, CI/CD triggers
260: 
261: #### Output Capture
262: 
263: Hooks can access agent output through template variables:
264: 
265: - `$agentOutput`: File content generated by triggering agent
266: - `$clipboardContent`: Clipboard content at execution time  
267: - `$outputPath`: Path to generated file
268: - Automatic capture after agent execution
269: - Graceful fallback to empty strings when unavailable
270: 
271: #### Template Variables
272: 
273: All standard template variables available in hooks:
274: 
275: - Standard: `$timestamp`, `$branch`, `$user`
276: - Spec-specific: `$specId`, `$specTitle`, `$specStatus`
277: - Output capture: `$agentOutput`, `$clipboardContent`, `$outputPath`
278: 
279: ### Implementation Details
280: 
281: **Backend Components**:
282: 
283: - `hook-executor.ts`: Executes hooks with timing support and output capture
284: - `trigger-registry.ts`: Fires triggers with timing parameter
285: - `command-completion-detector.ts`: Detects completion and captures output
286: - `template-variable-parser.ts`: Parses output capture variables
287: 
288: **Frontend Components**:
289: 
290: - `trigger-action-selector.tsx`: UI for timing selection and wait-for-completion
291: - `hook-list-item.tsx`: Displays timing badges and blocking indicators
292: - `argument-template-editor.tsx`: Variable picker with categorized output variables
293: 
294: **Configuration**:
295: 
296: - Hooks stored in workspace state (`.vscode/gatomia/hooks.json`)
297: - Timing defaults to "after" for backward compatibility
298: - Migration logic handles hooks without timing field
299: 
300: ### Testing
301: 
302: - Output variable parsing: 8 tests in `template-variable-parser.test.ts`
303: - Timing execution: Tests in `hook-executor.test.ts`
304: - Integration: Tests in `command-completion-detector.test.ts`
305: 
306: ## Troubleshooting
307: 
308: ### Tests Failing with "Cannot resolve import"
309: 
310: - Ensure UI dependencies installed: `npm run install:all`
311: - Check `vitest.config.ts` aliases are correct
312: - UI deps should be in `ui/node_modules/`
313: 
314: ### Extension Not Loading
315: 
316: - Build extension: `npm run build:ext`
317: - Check output channel: "GatomIA" in VS Code
318: - Verify VS Code version >= 1.84.0
319: 
320: ### Webview Not Rendering
321: 
322: - Build webview: `npm run build:webview`
323: - Check browser console in webview (Ctrl+Shift+I)
324: - Verify bridge communication in extension logs

97: 			})

99: 		this.subscriptions.push(
100: 			this.registry.onDidChange(() => {
101: 				this.emitChanged();
102: 			})
103: 		);
104: 		if (options.onCloudSessionsChanged) {

120: 	onDidChange(listener: () => void): { dispose(): void } {
121: 		this.listeners.add(listener);

129: 	async snapshot(): Promise<OrchestrationSnapshot> {
130: 		const sessions: OrchestrationSessionProjection[] = [];
131: 		const degradedReasons: string[] = [];
132: 		const cloudProviderRegistryAvailable =
133: 			this.cloudProviderRegistry !== undefined;
134: 		const cloudProviderCount = this.readCloudProviderCount();
135: 
136: 		const [agentChatSessions, cloudSessions] = await Promise.all([
137: 			this.collectAgentChatSessions(degradedReasons),
138: 			this.collectCloudSessions(degradedReasons),
139: 		]);
140: 
141: 		sessions.push(...agentChatSessions, ...cloudSessions);
142: 		sessions.sort(compareSessions);
143: 
144: 		const activeProvider = this.readActiveProvider(degradedReasons);
145: 
146: 		return {
147: 			sessions,
148: 			cloudProviderRegistryAvailable,
149: 			cloudProviderCount,
150: 			activeProvider,
151: 			generatedAt: this.now(),
152: 			degradedReasons,
153: 		};
154: 	}

156: 	dispose(): void {
157: 		for (const subscription of this.subscriptions) {
158: 			try {
159: 				subscription.dispose();
160: 			} catch {
161: 				// best-effort
162: 			}
163: 		}
164: 		this.subscriptions.length = 0;
165: 		this.listeners.clear();
166: 	}

168: 	private emitChanged(): void {
169: 		for (const listener of [...this.listeners]) {
170: 			try {
171: 				listener();
172: 			} catch {
173: 				// best-effort
174: 			}
175: 		}
176: 	}

178: 	private async collectAgentChatSessions(
179: 		degradedReasons: string[]
180: 	): Promise<OrchestrationSessionProjection[]> {
181: 		let active: AgentChatSession[] = [];
182: 		let recent: AgentChatSession[] = [];
183: 		try {
184: 			[active, recent] = await Promise.all([
185: 				this.store.listActive(),
186: 				this.store.listRecent(),
187: 			]);
188: 		} catch {
189: 			degradedReasons.push("Local agent session state could not be read.");
190: 			return [];
191: 		}
192: 		const byId = new Map<string, AgentChatSession>();
193: 		for (const session of [...active, ...recent]) {
194: 			byId.set(session.id, session);
195: 		}
196: 		for (const session of [
197: 			...this.registry.listActive(),
198: 			...this.registry.listRecent(),
199: 		]) {
200: 			byId.set(session.id, session);
201: 		}
202: 		return [...byId.values()].map((session) =>
203: 			toAgentChatProjection(session, this.readTranscript(session.id))
204: 		);
205: 	}

207: 	private async collectCloudSessions(
208: 		degradedReasons: string[]
209: 	): Promise<OrchestrationSessionProjection[]> {
210: 		if (!this.cloudSessionStorage) {
211: 			degradedReasons.push("Cloud agent session storage is unavailable.");
212: 			return [];
213: 		}
214: 		let sessions: AgentSession[] = [];
215: 		try {
216: 			sessions = await this.cloudSessionStorage.getAll();
217: 		} catch {
218: 			degradedReasons.push("Cloud agent status could not be read.");
219: 			return [];
220: 		}
221: 		return sessions.map((session) =>
222: 			toCloudProjection(session, this.cloudProviderRegistry)
223: 		);
224: 	}
225: 
226: 	private readActiveProvider(degradedReasons: string[]):
227: 		| {
228: 				readonly id: string;
229: 				readonly displayName: string;
230: 		  }
231: 		| undefined {
232: 		if (!this.cloudProviderRegistry) {
233: 			degradedReasons.push(
234: 				"Cloud agent provider wiring is unavailable. Open Agent Chat or refresh Cloud Agents after the provider surface is restored."
235: 			);
236: 			return;
237: 		}
238: 
239: 		const providers = this.cloudProviderRegistry.getAll();
240: 		if (providers.length === 0) {
241: 			return;
242: 		}
243: 
244: 		const activeProvider = this.cloudProviderRegistry.getActive();
245: 		if (!activeProvider) {
246: 			return;
247: 		}
248: 
249: 		return {
250: 			id: activeProvider.metadata.id,
251: 			displayName: activeProvider.metadata.displayName,
252: 		};
253: 	}
254: 
255: 	private readCloudProviderCount(): number {
256: 		if (!this.cloudProviderRegistry) {
257: 			return 0;
258: 		}
259: 
260: 		try {
261: 			return this.cloudProviderRegistry.getAll().length;
262: 		} catch {
263: 			return 0;
264: 		}
265: 	}
266: 
267: 	private readTranscript(sessionId: string): ChatMessage[] {
268: 		const workspaceState = (
269: 			this.store as unknown as {
270: 				workspaceState?: { get<T>(key: string): T | undefined };
271: 			}
272: 		).workspaceState;
273: 		if (!workspaceState) {
274: 			return [];
275: 		}
276: 		const raw = workspaceState.get<{ messages: ChatMessage[] }>(
277: 			transcriptKeyFor(sessionId)
278: 		);
279: 		return raw?.messages ?? [];
280: 	}
281: }
282: 
283: function toAgentChatProjection(
284: 	session: AgentChatSession,
285: 	transcript: readonly ChatMessage[]
286: ): OrchestrationSessionProjection {
287: 	const title = deriveAgentChatTitle(session, transcript);
288: 	const lastVisibleActivityAt = deriveLastVisibleActivityAt(
289: 		session,
290: 		transcript
291: 	);
292: 	return {
293: 		id: `agent-chat:${session.id}`,
294: 		source: "agent-chat",
295: 		sourceSessionId: session.id,
296: 		title,
297: 		agentName: session.agentDisplayName,
298: 		state: session.lifecycleState,
299: 		bucket: bucketForAgentChat(session.lifecycleState),
300: 		createdAt: session.createdAt,
301: 		updatedAt: session.updatedAt,
302: 		endedAt: session.endedAt,
303: 		lastVisibleActivityAt,
304: 		isBlocked: session.lifecycleState === "waiting-for-input",
305: 		worktreeStatus: session.worktree?.status,
306: 		executionTargetLabel: executionTargetLabel(session.executionTarget.kind),
307: 		externalUrl: session.cloud?.externalUrl,
308: 		cloudProviderId: session.cloud?.providerId,
309: 		openSessionCommand: { kind: "agent-chat", sessionId: session.id },
310: 	};
311: }
312: 
313: function toCloudProjection(
314: 	session: AgentSession,
315: 	providerRegistry: ProviderRegistry | undefined
316: ): OrchestrationSessionProjection {
317: 	const firstTask = session.tasks[0];
318: 	const title = firstTask
319: 		? `${firstTask.specTaskId}: ${firstTask.title}`
320: 		: session.specPath;
321: 	const providerDisplayName =
322: 		providerRegistry?.get(session.providerId)?.metadata.displayName ??
323: 		session.providerId;
324: 	const lastVisibleActivityAt = firstTask?.startedAt ?? session.updatedAt;
325: 	return {
326: 		id: `cloud-agent:${session.localId}`,
327: 		source: "cloud-agent",
328: 		sourceSessionId: session.localId,
329: 		title,
330: 		agentName: providerDisplayName,
331: 		state: session.status,
332: 		bucket: bucketForCloud(session.status),
333: 		createdAt: session.createdAt,
334: 		updatedAt: session.updatedAt,
335: 		endedAt: session.completedAt,
336: 		lastVisibleActivityAt,
337: 		isBlocked: session.status === SessionStatus.BLOCKED,
338: 		worktreeStatus: session.isReadOnly ? "read-only" : undefined,
339: 		executionTargetLabel: "Cloud",
340: 		externalUrl: session.externalUrl,
341: 		cloudProviderId: session.providerId,
342: 		openSessionCommand: {
343: 			kind: "cloud-agent",
344: 			localId: session.localId,
345: 			externalUrl: session.externalUrl,
346: 		},
347: 	};
348: }
349: 
350: function deriveAgentChatTitle(
351: 	session: AgentChatSession,
352: 	transcript: readonly ChatMessage[]
353: ): string {
354: 	const promptTitle = firstUserMessageText(transcript);
355: 	if (promptTitle) {
356: 		return promptTitle;
357: 	}
358: 	if (session.selectedModeId) {
359: 		return `${session.agentDisplayName} (${session.selectedModeId})`;
360: 	}
361: 	return session.agentDisplayName;
362: }
363: 
364: function deriveLastVisibleActivityAt(
365: 	session: AgentChatSession,
366: 	transcript: readonly ChatMessage[]
367: ): number {
368: 	let latest = session.updatedAt;
369: 	for (const message of transcript) {
370: 		if (message.timestamp > latest) {
371: 			latest = message.timestamp;
372: 		}
373: 	}
374: 	return latest;
375: }
376: 
377: function firstUserMessageText(
378: 	transcript: readonly ChatMessage[]
379: ): string | undefined {
380: 	for (const message of transcript) {
381: 		if (message.role !== "user") {
382: 			continue;
383: 		}
384: 		const content = normalizePromptTitle(message);
385: 		if (content) {
386: 			return content;
387: 		}
388: 	}
389: 	return;
390: }
391: 
392: function normalizePromptTitle(message: UserChatMessage): string | undefined {
393: 	const text = message.content.trim().replace(/\s+/g, " ");
394: 	return text.length > 0 ? text : undefined;
395: }
396: 
397: function bucketForAgentChat(
398: 	state: SessionLifecycleState
399: ): OrchestrationSessionBucket {
400: 	if (state === "waiting-for-input") {
401: 		return "waiting";
402: 	}
403: 	if (state === "completed") {
404: 		return "completed";
405: 	}
406: 	if (
407: 		state === "failed" ||
408: 		state === "cancelled" ||
409: 		state === "ended-by-shutdown"
410: 	) {
411: 		return "failed";
412: 	}
413: 	return "active";
414: }
415: 
416: function bucketForCloud(
417: 	status: AgentSession["status"]
418: ): OrchestrationSessionBucket {
419: 	if (status === SessionStatus.BLOCKED) {
420: 		return "waiting";
421: 	}
422: 	if (status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED) {
423: 		return "completed";
424: 	}
425: 	if (status === SessionStatus.FAILED || status === SessionStatus.CANCELLED) {
426: 		return "failed";
427: 	}
428: 	return "active";
429: }
430: 
431: function executionTargetLabel(kind: "local" | "worktree" | "cloud"): string {
432: 	if (kind === "worktree") {
433: 		return "Worktree";
434: 	}
435: 	if (kind === "cloud") {
436: 		return "Cloud";
437: 	}
438: 	return "Local";
439: }
440: 
441: function compareSessions(
442: 	a: OrchestrationSessionProjection,
443: 	b: OrchestrationSessionProjection
444: ): number {
445: 	if (a.bucket !== b.bucket) {
446: 		return bucketRank(a.bucket) - bucketRank(b.bucket);
447: 	}
448: 	return b.lastVisibleActivityAt - a.lastVisibleActivityAt;
449: }
450: 
451: function bucketRank(bucket: OrchestrationSessionBucket): number {
452: 	if (bucket === "active") {
453: 		return 0;
454: 	}
455: 	if (bucket === "waiting") {
456: 		return 1;
457: 	}
458: 	if (bucket === "completed") {
459: 		return 2;
460: 	}
461: 	return 3;
462: }

1: import {
2: 	commands,
3: 	env,
4: 	type CancellationToken,
5: 	type Disposable,
6: 	type ExtensionContext,
7: 	type WebviewView,
8: 	type WebviewViewProvider,
9: 	type WebviewViewResolveContext,
10: 	Uri,
11: } from "vscode";
12: import {
13: 	AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS,
14: 	logTelemetry,
15: } from "../features/agent-chat/telemetry";
16: import type {
17: 	OrchestrationReadModel,
18: 	OrchestrationSnapshot,
19: 	OrchestrationSessionProjection,
20: } from "../features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model";
21: import { getWebviewContent } from "../utils/get-webview-content";
22: 
23: interface OrchestrationViewProviderOptions {
24: 	readonly context: ExtensionContext;
25: 	readonly readModel: OrchestrationReadModel;
26: 	readonly outputChannel?: { appendLine(value: string): void };
27: }
28: 
29: export class OrchestrationViewProvider implements WebviewViewProvider {
30: 	static readonly viewType = "gatomia.views.orchestration";
31: 
32: 	private readonly context: ExtensionContext;
33: 	private readonly readModel: OrchestrationReadModel;
34: 	private readonly outputChannel?: { appendLine(value: string): void };
35: 	private readonly disposables: Disposable[] = [];
36: 	private view: WebviewView | undefined;
37: 
38: 	constructor(options: OrchestrationViewProviderOptions) {
39: 		this.context = options.context;
40: 		this.readModel = options.readModel;
41: 		this.outputChannel = options.outputChannel;
42: 
43: 		this.disposables.push(
44: 			this.readModel.onDidChange(() => {
45: 				this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
46: 					this.reportError("snapshot push", error);
47: 				});
48: 			})
49: 		);
50: 	}
51: 
52: 	resolveWebviewView(
53: 		webviewView: WebviewView,
54: 		_context: WebviewViewResolveContext,
55: 		_token: CancellationToken
56: 	): void {
57: 		this.view = webviewView;
58: 		logTelemetry(AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS.PANEL_OPENED, {
59: 			source: "orchestration",
60: 			surface: "orchestration",
61: 		});
62: 
63: 		webviewView.webview.options = {
64: 			enableScripts: true,
65: 			localResourceRoots: [this.context.extensionUri],
66: 		};
67: 		webviewView.webview.html = getWebviewContent(
68: 			webviewView.webview,
69: 			this.context.extensionUri,
70: 			"orchestration"
71: 		);
72: 
73: 		this.disposables.push(
74: 			webviewView.webview.onDidReceiveMessage((message) => {
75: 				this.handleWebviewMessage(message).catch((error) => {
76: 					this.reportError("message handling", error);
77: 				});
78: 			})
79: 		);
80: 
81: 		this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
82: 			this.reportError("initial snapshot", error);
83: 		});
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	async reveal(): Promise<void> {
87: 		await commands.executeCommand(
88: 			`${OrchestrationViewProvider.viewType}.focus`
89: 		);
90: 		this.view?.show?.(true);
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	refresh(): void {
94: 		this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
95: 			this.reportError("refresh", error);
96: 		});
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	dispose(): void {
100: 		for (const disposable of this.disposables) {
101: 			try {
102: 				disposable.dispose();
103: 			} catch {
104: 				// best-effort
105: 			}
106: 		}
107: 		this.disposables.length = 0;
108: 	}
109: 
110: 	private async handleWebviewMessage(raw: unknown): Promise<void> {
111: 		const message = raw as { type?: string; payload?: unknown };
112: 		if (!message?.type) {
113: 			return;
114: 		}
115: 
116: 		switch (message.type) {
117: 			case "orchestration/ready":
118: 				await this.pushSnapshot();
119: 				return;
120: 			case "orchestration/refresh":
121: 				logTelemetry("orchestration.refresh.requested", {
122: 					surface: "orchestration",
123: 				});
124: 				await this.focusTreeView("gatomia.views.cloudAgents").catch(() => {
125: 					// best-effort
126: 				});
127: 				await commands
128: 					.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents")
129: 					.catch(() => {
130: 						// best-effort
131: 					});
132: 				await this.pushSnapshot();
133: 				return;
134: 			case "orchestration/open-session": {
135: 				const sessionId = (
136: 					message.payload as { sessionId?: string } | undefined
137: 				)?.sessionId;
138: 				if (!sessionId) {
139: 					return;
140: 				}
141: 				const snapshot = await this.readModel.snapshot();
142: 				const session = snapshot.sessions.find(
143: 					(entry) => entry.id === sessionId
144: 				);
145: 				if (session) {
146: 					await this.openSession(session);
147: 				}
148: 				return;
149: 			}
150: 			case "orchestration/open-existing-surface": {
151: 				const source = (message.payload as { source?: string } | undefined)
152: 					?.source;
153: 				logTelemetry("orchestration.surface.open-existing", {
154: 					source: source ?? "unknown",
155: 				});
156: 				if (source === "cloud-agent") {
157: 					await this.focusTreeView("gatomia.views.cloudAgents").catch(() => {
158: 						// best-effort
159: 					});
160: 					await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents");
161: 					return;
162: 				}
163: 				await this.focusTreeView("gatomia.views.runningAgents").catch(() => {
164: 					// best-effort
165: 				});
166: 				await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.agentChat.openPanel");
167: 				return;
168: 			}
169: 			case "orchestration/open-external": {
170: 				const url = (message.payload as { url?: string } | undefined)?.url;
171: 				if (url) {
172: 					logTelemetry("orchestration.external.opened", {
173: 						surface: "orchestration",
174: 					});
175: 					await env.openExternal(Uri.parse(url));
176: 				}
177: 				return;
178: 			}
179: 			default:
180: 				return;
181: 		}
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	private async pushSnapshot(): Promise<void> {
185: 		if (!this.view) {
186: 			return;
187: 		}
188: 		let snapshot: OrchestrationSnapshot;
189: 		try {
190: 			snapshot = await this.readModel.snapshot();
191: 		} catch (error) {
192: 			this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
193: 				`[Orchestration] snapshot read failed: ${formatError(error)}`
194: 			);
195: 			logTelemetry("orchestration.snapshot.failed", {
196: 				surface: "orchestration",
197: 			});
198: 			snapshot = {
199: 				sessions: [],
200: 				cloudProviderRegistryAvailable: false,
201: 				cloudProviderCount: 0,
202: 				activeProvider: undefined,
203: 				generatedAt: Date.now(),
204: 				degradedReasons: [
205: 					"Failed to read orchestration status. Refresh the view or check the output channel.",
206: 				],
207: 			};
208: 		}
209: 		await this.view.webview.postMessage({
210: 			type: "orchestration/snapshot",
211: 			payload: snapshot,
212: 		});
213: 	}
214: 
215: 	private async openSession(
216: 		session: OrchestrationSessionProjection
217: 	): Promise<void> {
218: 		logTelemetry(AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS.PANEL_OPENED, {
219: 			sessionId: session.sourceSessionId,
220: 			source: session.source,
221: 			surface: "orchestration",
222: 		});
223: 
224: 		if (session.openSessionCommand.kind === "agent-chat") {
225: 			await commands.executeCommand(
226: 				"gatomia.agentChat.openForSession",
227: 				session.openSessionCommand.sessionId
228: 			);
229: 			return;
230: 		}
231: 
232: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
233: 			await env.openExternal(Uri.parse(session.externalUrl));
234: 			return;
235: 		}
236: 
237: 		await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents");
238: 	}
239: 
240: 	private async focusTreeView(viewId: string): Promise<void> {
241: 		await commands.executeCommand(`${viewId}.focus`);
242: 	}
243: 
244: 	private reportError(operation: string, error: unknown): void {
245: 		const message = formatError(error);
246: 		this.outputChannel?.appendLine(

# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when a beads workspace is resolved

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)
- `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans)

### Quality Tools
- `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections
- `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate)
- `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions
- `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes
- `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create
- `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections

### Lifecycle & Hygiene
- `bd defer <id> --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date
- `bd supersede <id> --with=<new-id>` - Mark issue as superseded
- `bd close <id> --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing
- `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity
- `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies
- `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans)
- `bd human <id>` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss)

### Structured Workflows
- `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates
- `bd mol pour <name>` - Start structured workflow from formula

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```
